sections.heading: Why Assess Your Communication Skills?
sections.body: You can't improve what you don't measure. A communication skills assessment helps you identify your strengths to leverage and your blind spots to address. Most people have 1-2 dominant communication styles and 1-2 areas they neglect.

sections.heading: The Four Communication Dimensions
bulletPoints: Speaking — Do you speak clearly, confidently, and concisely?
Listening — Do you actively listen, or do you wait for your turn to talk?
Writing — Is your written communication clear, professional, and actionable?
Nonverbal — Does your body language match and reinforce your verbal message?

sections.heading: Quick Self-Assessment (1-10 Scale)
bulletPoints: Speaking: How clear and confident am I in meetings and conversations?
Listening: How often do I paraphrase, ask clarifying questions, and resist interrupting?
Writing: Are my emails and messages scannable, specific, and actionable?
Nonverbal: Does my body language project confidence and openness?
Feedback: How comfortable am I giving and receiving honest feedback?
Empathy: How often do I seek to understand before being understood?

jargon.definition: A systematic review of your communication habits across all channels (speaking, listening, writing, nonverbal) to identify strengths and gaps.
A communication weakness you're unaware of. Blind spots are only visible through feedback from others or self-assessment tools.
A focused development plan that targets 1-2 specific communication skills for improvement over 30-90 days.

summary: Assess your communication across four dimensions: speaking, listening, writing, and nonverbal. Rate yourself honestly on a 1-10 scale. Identify your top strength to leverage and your biggest gap to address. Focus on 1-2 areas at a time — trying to improve everything at once changes nothing.
